title: Pilbara Minerals Reports Record Spodumene Production in Q1 FY2026
snippet: Pilbara Minerals Ltd (PLS.ASX) announced record quarterly spodumene concentrate production of 260,000 tonnes at its Pilgangoora operation, driven by the P680 expansion ramp-up. Cash costs declined to US$420/t, positioning PLS as one of the lowest-cost lithium producers globally.

title: Lithium Prices Climb Above US$1,100/t — First Time Since 2023
snippet: Spodumene 6% concentrate (CIF China) breached US$1,100/tonne for the first time since early 2023, reflecting a tighter supply-demand balance. BMI analysts cite EV battery demand growth of 25% YoY and slower-than-expected new supply from Africa as key drivers.

title: WA Government Fast-Tracks Pilbara Lithium Infrastructure
snippet: The Western Australian government committed AU$350M to accelerate Pilbara lithium infrastructure, including a new 220kV transmission line to the Pilgangoora-Wodgina corridor and upgrades to Port Hedland bulk-handling capacity. The investment is expected to reduce logistics costs by 12-15%.

title: Liontown's Kathleen Valley Reaches 80% Nameplate Capacity
snippet: Liontown Resources announced Kathleen Valley's processing plant has reached 80% of its 500ktpa nameplate capacity, with full ramp-up expected by September 2026. DMS circuit recoveries have consistently exceeded 75% in the past two quarters.

title: MinRes Suspends Bald Hill Lithium Operation Indefinitely
snippet: Mineral Resources placed its Bald Hill lithium mine on care & maintenance, citing unsustainably low spodumene prices at the time. ~300 jobs affected. The company states the mine can be restarted within 6 months if prices recover above US$1,200/t.

title: China's Lithium Carbonate Imports from Australia Surge 35% YoY
snippet: Chinese customs data shows lithium carbonate imports from Australian converters rose 35% YoY in Q1 2026, reflecting strong downstream demand from LFP battery cathode makers. Tianqi's Kwinana plant and IGO's Kwinana JV both reported record output.

title: Pilbara Region Lithium Output to Exceed 3Mtpa by 2027 — BMI Forecast
snippet: BMI forecasts total Pilbara-region spodumene output will surpass 3 million tonnes per annum by 2027, up from ~1.8Mt in 2025. Key contributors: Pilgangoora expansion to 1Mt, Wodgina full restart, and Andover greenfield. However, permitting risks remain for greenfield projects.

title: EU Critical Raw Materials Act Triggers Scramble for Australian Lithium Offtake
snippet: European automakers and cathode producers are aggressively pursuing long-term offtake agreements with Australian lithium miners, driven by EU Critical Raw Materials Act requirements. Pilbara Minerals and Liontown both confirmed advanced negotiations with European buyers, diversifying away from Chinese-dominated offtake.

title: MinRes-Albemarle Wodgina JV Expansion Faces Cost Overruns
snippet: The Wodgina JV's Train 3 restart has encountered AU$80M in cost overruns, pushing the projected restart cost to AU$450M. MinRes attributes delays to contractor shortages and escalating structural steel costs. Albemarle has approved the revised budget.

title: Lithium Market Deficit Expected by 2028 — Goldman Sachs
snippet: Goldman Sachs projects the lithium market will swing into a structural deficit by 2028 (~50kt LCE), revising its earlier surplus forecast. The bank cites slower African ramp-up, faster EV penetration (28% of new car sales globally by 2028), and accelerating energy storage system (ESS) deployment as the key factors.

title: Rio Tinto Enters Pilbara Lithium with Andover Project FID
snippet: Rio Tinto board gave final investment decision (FID) to the Andover lithium project in the Pilbara, committing US$1.5Bn to build a 500ktpa spodumene concentrator. First production is targeted for H2 2028. The move intensifies competition for labor and infrastructure in the Pilbara.

title: Albemarle Considers Reducing Kemerton Lithium Hydroxide Output
snippet: Albemarle is reviewing its Kemerton lithium hydroxide plant in WA, with potential temporary curtailment of Train 1 due to persistent negative conversion margins. The plant's 50ktpa total capacity may be reduced to 30ktpa until hydroxide prices recover.

title: Australian Lithium Export Revenue to Hit AU$25Bn in FY2027 — Government Forecast
snippet: Australia's Resources and Energy Quarterly (REQ) forecasts lithium export revenue will reach AU$25 billion in FY2027, surpassing thermal coal. Spodumene exports are projected at 4.2Mt, supported by recent mine expansions and new capacity from Kathleen Valley.

title: Bald Hill Restart Under Review as Lithium Prices Recover
snippet: Mineral Resources confirmed it has initiated an internal review of Bald Hill's restart economics. With spodumene prices recovering above US$1,000/t, the company sees a potential decision window in Q3 2026. Bald Hill produced 120kt of spodumene in 2024 before its suspension.

title: Pilbara Port Hedland Lithium Export Capacity to Double
snippet: Port Hedland Port Authority approved Stage 2 of its lithium export berth expansion, doubling annual lithium concentrate export capacity to 4 million tonnes. Construction starts Q3 2026, targeting completion by mid-2027.
